architecture.summary: Patchscopes is a framework for inspecting hidden representations of LLMs by leveraging the model itself to generate natural-language explanations. The core mechanism patches a copy of the model's computation at a chosen layer, replacing downstream layers with a query function that asks the model to explain what a particular representation means. Information flows from early/intermediate layer activations through a patched forward pass; the model's text generation capability is used as the explanation engine. The framework unifies prior methods (vocabulary projection, intervention techniques) while enabling new interpretability modes such as cross-model explanation (using a capable model to explain a smaller model's representations) and error correction via multihop reasoning.

biblio.title: Patchscopes: A Unifying Framework for Inspecting Hidden Representations of Language Models

claims.statement: Patchscopes can be used to answer a wide range of questions about an LLM's computation
Many prior interpretability methods based on projecting representations into the vocabulary space and intervening on the LLM computation can be viewed as instances of the Patchscopes framework
Patchscopes can mitigate shortcomings of prior methods such as failure in inspecting early layers or lack of expressivity
Patchscopes enables using a more capable model to explain the representations of a smaller model
Patchscopes enables multihop reasoning error correction
